task_completion.description: Agent opened the form to create a new namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows a 'Create Namespace' form or page.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct namespace name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Name' input field in the namespace creation form contains the text 'production'.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct label key
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Labels section (or Labels & Annotations tab), a label key input field contains the text 'environment'.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct label value
task_completion.visual_evidence: Next to the 'environment' key, the corresponding label value input field contains the text 'production'.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully created the namespace and it appears in the list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Namespaces list/overview page is visible, and 'production' is displayed as an existing namespace in the table.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rancher Web UI rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions and results are shown within a web browser displaying the Rancher UI, with no terminal windows or 'kubectl' commands used to create the namespace.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to fake the output
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen does not show a text editor (like Notepad, gedit, or nano) being used to type out the namespace name or labels.

integrity.description: Results appear in the genuine Rancher application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Rancher dashboard layout with appropriate navigation menus, headers, and table structures, not a mocked or locally edited HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target namespace name is 'production'. The namespace must be assigned a label with the key 'environment' and the value 'production'.
